content.full_text:
RENE AUGUSTE CHOUTEAU
BRUCE LEAVITT
BRIEN J. FARRELL
MATTHEW J. LeBLANC
THERESA L. CUNNINGHAM

January 23, 1992

SENT VIA FAX


John W. Wallace, Counsel
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812

Dear Mr. Wallace:

At the request of, and on behalf of, Bonnie Band, Investment Analyst for the City
of Santa Rosa, I request a "conflict of interest" determination based upon the
following information.

Ms. Band has been involved in the investment of City funds since she started to
work for the City of Santa Rosa in 1983. She is not a management employee. The
City Treasurer, John Lindsay, has ultimate responsibility for the decisions made
for the investment and treasury management programs of the City of Santa Rosa.

Ms. Band has recently been offered a part-time position as Cash Handler in the
Special Vault Department of the Bank of America. In this position, Ms. Band
would be responsible for processing Versateller transactions, including deposits
and loan payments. She would not be responsible for processing any City of Santa
Rosa deposits.

In her position as Investment Analyst for the City of Santa Rosa, Ms. Band is
responsible for day-to-day investment of inactive reserves. Investments are made
in accordance with standards imposed by Government Code Section 53635, et seq.,
and the investment policy of the City of Santa Rosa. This policy is adopted by
resolution by the City Council and reviewed annually. Her duties include
determination of funds available and the subsequent negotiation of securities to
best meet the cash flow needs and investment objectives of the City. As the
staff member primarily responsible for treasury management, she is the City's
liaison to its bank, the Bank of America. In this capacity, she is responsible
for transferring funds between accounts, maintaining appropriate compensating
balances and resolving routine problems.


OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
100 Santa Rosa Avenue Post Office Box 1678 Santa Rosa California 95402-1678
Telephone 707-524-5261 FAX 707-524-5017

John W. Wallace
Page 2
January 23, 1992


Transactions with the Special Vault Department of the Bank of America are limited
to deposit reconcilement and infrequent purchases of large amounts of currency
for use as police department "flash" money.

The City's relationship with the Bank of America is multifaceted. The City works
with the Trust, Commercial Banking, and Investment Divisions of the Bank of
America. Ms. Band purchases securities through four primary securities dealers,
one of which is the Bank of America, via a competitive bidding process.
Securities are selected based on yield, maturity, and creditworthiness of the
issuer. As of December 31, 1991, less than 20% of the City's $124,000,000
investment holdings were purchased through the Bank of America. Specific
purchases and sales made by Ms. Band are approved after the purchase or sale by
the City's Financial Analyst. Additionally, an investment management committee
meets bi-weekly to review the portfolio, discuss economic news, and recommend
changes of current policies or strategies.

The City is now considering putting out to bid a contract for banking services.
A committee comprised of City staff, including Ms. Band, is charged with
responsibility for evaluating the advisability of soliciting bids. If there is
a determination to request bids for banking services, Ms. Band would be part of
a committee that would analyze responses and make recommendations. Selection of
a bank would be based on the ability of that institution to provide the range of
services required by the City, as well as its fee structure.

Your prompt attention to this request for advice would be much appreciated. Ms.
Band would like to begin work the week of January 27, 1992, if there is no legal
obstacle. Thank you very much for your courtesy and cooperation.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
BRIEN J. FARRELL
Assistant City Attorney

BJF:mp
